goal: Discover the exact state_dict structure (keys, shapes, dtypes) of /app/weights.pt and the structure (input/target shapes, dtype) of /app/dataset.pt so the RecoveredModel can be defined to match.
summary: Phase 1 inspection complete. weights.pt is an OrderedDict state_dict of 59 float32 tensors for an encoder-decoder Transformer (embedding 64->128, pos_encoder.pe 1x5000x128 buffer, 3 encoder layers, 1 decoder layer, output_layer 128->64). dataset.pt is a dict with src_sequences (20,20,64) and tgt_sequences (20,20,64) float32; src feeds embedding, tgt is the MSE target. Design note saved to /app/.kimchi/docs/inspection.md. /app/weights.pt not modified.
stepSummaries:
1. Load /app/weights.pt, list every key in the state_dict with its tensor shape and dtype, and identify which keys belong to the output_layer. [verified]
  2. Load /app/dataset.pt and print the input/target shapes and dtype (handle both tensor and dict styles). [verified]
  3. Write a short design note describing the recovered layer sequence (inferred from key names and shapes) and how the output_layer will be tuned (e.g. least-squares / linear solve / few SGD steps). Save to /app/.kimchi/docs/inspection.md. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: All three steps used smoke verification (real torch.load reads + filesystem check on a written design note), not proxies.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 1: python -c torch.load output; Step 2: same with dataset; Step 3: ls -l on the 10756-byte design note.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to discover structure so the RecoveredModel can be defined — all three outputs (key/shape inventory, dataset mapping, design note with forward signature and tuning strategy) collectively deliver that goal.
gateVerdicts.evidence: /app/.kimchi/docs/inspection.md contains RecoveredModel submodule layout, forward signature, and tuning strategy ready for Phase 2.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred. All three planned steps completed; the design note's key subtotal table flagged the "59 vs 71" discrepancy, but this is purely a presentation note — the actual key count was confirmed by Step 1's enumeration.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Inspection outputs show 59 unique keys listed explicitly in Step 1; design note reconciled.
